topic.what: game-theoretic incentive mechanisms and RTT-based location verification to promote geographic diversity in permissionless blockchain networks
topic.how: 2-tier reward scheme with deposits, formal geostable graph model, distributed localization algorithm, Nash equilibrium and collusion-resistance proofs
topic.why_matters: geographic concentration of nodes creates systemic vulnerabilities to geopolitical events, censorship, natural disasters, and adverse regulations

characteristics.what_it_measures: probability p(t,ts) that spoofing nodes are correctly identified by localization algorithm
characteristics.value: varies with k, coalition size, player count
characteristics.evidence: Figure 3: detection probability increases with player count; decreases with larger coalitions
characteristics.context: k=t+1 for zero false positives; data set 2 (152 nodes)

characteristics.what_it_measures: probability ph(t,ts) that honest nodes are incorrectly flagged as malicious
characteristics.evidence: Figure 2: ph drops to zero when k exceeds number of spoofers
characteristics.context: controlled by threshold parameter k in Algorithm 1

characteristics.what_itasures: maximum geographic distance a node can spoof its location without detection, expressed as fraction of network diameter
characteristics.evidence: Figure 1: spoofing distance increases with number of spoofers in coalition
characteristics.context: t=3 coalition, k in {1,2,3} across both data sets (132 and 152 nodes)

characteristics.what_itasures: relationship between deposit d, base reward r, and geographic bonus g for Nash equilibrium
characteristics.evidence: Theorem 1 proof establishes incentive compatibility condition

characteristics.what_itasures: minimum deposit/reward ratio required to resist coalitions of size t
characteristics.evidence: sufficient condition for all-or-nothing detection model
characteristics.context: assumes lenient protocol not penalizing helpers

characteristics.what_itasures: impact of jitter and network noise on detection rates
characteristics.evidence: Appendix G: limited effect on detection rates

novelty: first formal study of geographic diversity incentivization in permissionless systems
graph-theoretic geostability notion connecting to geodetic set
game-theoretic conditions for Nash equilibrium and collusion-resistance
distributed localization algorithm with tunable k threshold for false positive/detection tradeoff
open_problems: extending formal model to general collusion scenarios beyond single coalition
multi-tiered strategy space and richer action sets
dishes Honest players framing without direct reward gain
scalability analysis for large blockchain networks
